items.html: AI startups are rushing direct-to-video AI-generated films to digital release, mirroring B-movie economics — a new distribution lane video creators should watch.
Reelful's new iOS app auto-generates TikTok and Instagram Reels from your camera roll photos with no editing skills required.
Hugging Face's new Real World VoiceEQ benchmark measures human perception of voice AI output — a new quality bar for audio and voice creators to reference.
George Lucas says 'there's nothing you can do about AI' in filmmaking, comparing the shift to the end of horse transport — a signal of where industry consensus is heading.
Ghost Font is a new typeface engineered with optical illusions to defeat AI OCR and vision models — a practical tool for creators protecting on-screen text from scraping.

brief.title: Wednesday, July 15 — six things that changed today

tooldrop.name: Review by Eddie AI
tooldrop.blurb: AI-powered video review tool that lets editors and clients leave time-stamped feedback directly on footage — cuts the back-and-forth for video creators.

stories.title: The Best AI Artists Aren't Prompting. They're Directing.
stories.dek: Everyone with a browser can type a prompt. That stopped being impressive about five minutes after Midjourney launched. The real skill gap has moved somewhere else entirely.

stories.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines releases Inkling, an open 975B-param multimodal model
stories.dek: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, an open-weight mixture-of-experts model trained on 45 trillion tokens across text, image, audio, and video—reasoning natively across all modalities.

stories.title: Houdini 22 adds native Gaussian Splatting support and redesigned UI
stories.dek: SideFX's major release brings native 3D Gaussian Splat creation, editing, and animation without third-party tools, plus workflow upgrades for VFX, game dev, and procedural art.

stories.title: AI startups release direct-to-video generated films as cash-grab strategy
stories.dek: An AI startup is capitalizing on blockbuster hype by rushing out AI-generated films directly to digital release, mirroring decades-old direct-to-video B-movie economics.

stories.title: OpenAI releases $230 Codex keyboard with AI agent status lights
stories.dek: OpenAI entered hardware with a specialty keyboard co-designed with Work Louder, featuring customizable keys and a reasoning-level dial for managing AI coding agents.

stories.title: Style Transfer Isn't Theft, Except When It Obviously Is
stories.dek: Artists complaining that AI was trained on their work fall into two very different camps: those with a genuinely serious legal grievance, and those who are mostly upset that the market got harder. Lumping them together helps nobody.

stories.title: Suno trained on millions of scraped songs from YouTube, Deezer, Genius
stories.dek: Leaked data reveals the AI music generator sourced training material from major platforms without disclosed consent, raising fair use questions for creators and platforms.

stories.title: The Hands Problem Is Basically Solved. So What's Left to Distrust?
stories.dek: For two years, 'count the fingers' was the internet's favorite AI detector. That test is now mostly useless, and the real tells are stranger and more unsettling than an extra thumb.

stories.title: Shippy: building a high-stakes maritime AI agent
stories.dek: Ai2 details the architecture behind Shippy, an AI agent for maritime protection decisions. Covers agent anatomy, deterministic tools, sandboxed hosting, and evaluation.

stories.title: Model routing in agents: cost, complexity, and latency tradeoffs
stories.dek: IBM Research breaks down the real challenges of routing requests between different AI models in agent systems, beyond simple cost optimization.

stories.title: Hack reveals Suno scraped YouTube, Deezer, and podcasts for AI training
stories.dek: A supply chain attack exposed source code showing the AI music generator harvested audio from multiple platforms. Record labels argue this violates the DMCA despite Suno's fair-use defense.

stories.title: Microsoft patches record 570 vulnerabilities using AI-assisted discovery
stories.dek: Microsoft's monthly Patch Tuesday resolved a record number of security flaws, with at least two zero-day exploits, using AI to help identify code vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Hugging Face releases Real World VoiceEQ benchmark for voice AI quality
stories.dek: New metric measures human perception of voice AI output, advancing evaluation beyond specialized metrics as voice AI capabilities accelerate.

stories.title: OpenAI launches Codex Micro, a programmable keyboard for AI coding
stories.dek: Collaboration with Work Louder, the Codex Micro light-up button block monitors multiple AI coding threads, separate from OpenAI's rumored smart speaker project.

stories.title: Apple Intelligence launches in China via Alibaba Qwen partnership
stories.dek: China's regulator approved Apple Intelligence by integrating Alibaba's Qwen AI model into iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS, marking Apple's major expansion into a key market.

stories.title: Concept artist Daniel Porto accelerates 20 years of vision with AI
stories.dek: Porto leverages AI to collapse the gap between imagination and production, but emphasizes experienced judgment—not prompting—as the real competitive edge in creative work.

stories.title: Ode with Anthropic embeds forward-deployed AI engineers into enterprise
stories.dek: Ode, a joint venture backed by Anthropic and major financial firms, acquired Fractional AI to place specialized engineers inside enterprise organizations, betting AI services will replace traditional consulting.

stories.title: Anthropic launches $1.5B AI implementation venture with Blackstone
stories.dek: Ode pairs AI engineers with enterprise customers, betting that hands-on deployment—not just models—is the next trillion-dollar opportunity in AI adoption.

stories.title: Reelful automates short-form video creation from camera roll photos
stories.dek: New iOS app uses AI to turn photos and clips into polished TikTok and Instagram Reels, targeting users who find traditional editing tools too complex.

stories.title: Rime raises $24M Series A for enterprise voice AI customer support
stories.dek: Voice AI startup handling over 100 million calls monthly uses proprietary conversational training data to reduce customer customization burden for enterprises.

stories.title: Vint Cerf working on standards to identify AI agents on the open internet
stories.dek: The TCP/IP architect is advising Innovation Labs on creating an open architecture for AI agents to identify themselves using domain-name infrastructure.

stories.title: George Lucas: 'There's nothing you can do about AI' in filmmaking
stories.dek: Influential filmmaker compares AI's impact on cinema to the historical shift from horse transport to automobiles.

stories.title: AI image generation called 'pop culture's greatest tribute band'
stories.dek: Analysis of generative AI's tendency toward derivative cultural pastiche, examining why novelty in AI-made content feels exhausted.

stories.title: OpenAI releases GPT-Red, automated red-teaming system for AI safety
stories.dek: Self-play framework designed to stress-test AI robustness and resistance to prompt injection. Advances in safety methodology relevant to all generative systems.

stories.title: Ghost Font—an optical illusion font designed to confuse AI image readers
stories.dek: A typeface engineered with visual tricks intended to defeat AI OCR and vision models. Tests user effectiveness against AI text recognition.

stories.title: Inkling by Thinking Machines now available on Hugging Face
stories.dek: Inkling, the 1T-parameter multimodal open model, is now deployed with inference support via Transformers, vLLM, and llama.cpp for agentic coding and multi-token prediction tasks.

stories.title: OpenAI developing screenless AI speaker with physical movement
stories.dek: OpenAI's first hardware product is reportedly a mobile smart speaker with mechanical elements designed to function as a ChatGPT-powered home companion.

stories.title: OpenAI denies merit in Apple trade secret lawsuit
stories.dek: OpenAI responded to Apple's allegations that it poached employees to steal confidential information, stating the complaint lacks evidence.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol deleting user files without consent, widespread reports
stories.dek: Users report OpenAI's latest coding model is unexpectedly deleting files and databases without warning—a problem OpenAI disclosed in June.

stories.title: Apple releases iOS 27 public beta with revamped AI-powered Siri
stories.dek: Apple's redesigned Siri assistant is now available in public beta ahead of fall launch, marking the first broad test of its AI overhaul against competitors like ChatGPT and Gemini.
